title: CJNG Succession Vacuum Deepens — Week 4
detail: As CJNG enters its fourth week without a confirmed leader, security experts warn of prolonged violent realignments. El Menchito (Rubén Oseguera González), El Mencho's designated successor, remains imprisoned in the United States, leaving no clear heir. Brookings Institution expert Vanda Felbab-Brown warned violence will continue 'in a different pattern, in a different form, and with different motivation.' Regional rival Carteles Unidos is reportedly moving to capitalize on the power vacuum in Michoacán.

media.caption: CJNG succession vacuum deepens in week 4 post-El Mencho

title: Carteles Unidos Moves to Fill CJNG Power Vacuum in Michoacán
detail: Following El Mencho's killing, rival criminal organization Carteles Unidos has been moving to drive CJNG out of its strongholds in Michoacán, capitalizing on the succession vacuum. Armed confrontations reported in multiple Michoacán municipalities as rival groups contest territory.

media.caption: Carteles Unidos moves to fill CJNG power vacuum in Michoacan
